title: glandularia corymbosa
intro: Glandularia corymbosa, also known as Rosy Moss, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a shrubby perennial that is a member of the veronica family and is often used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. The herbal preparations of Glandularia corymbosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
